title: 1981-04-16_Pat_141.txt
key_claims: Relying on the *Sepher Yezirah*, Dick observes that to God, "Idea, word + work are one + the same." Therefore, the universe is literally composed of the thoughts of the creator in verbal/informational form.

The creator fell victim to his own creation, becoming imprisoned in his own thought-formations and forgetting his identity. The Cosmic Christ is a strategy employed by God to rescue Himself, awakening Him to His true identity (anamnesis) and causing Him to declare *Anokhi* ("I am").
Karma and Heimarmene are the "retribution machinery" through which the creator's prior thought-formations return to enslave him (the Bardo Thodol experience).
The "Maze" is a Chinese finger trap fed by the frantic thoughts of the creator trying to escape it.
recurring_concepts: **Bardo Thodol**: The Tibetan concept of the afterlife where one's own prior thoughts return as independent realities; Dick equates this to the experience of being trapped in the Maze.
reading_excerpt: Relying on the *Sepher Yezirah*, Dick observes that to God, "Idea, word + work are one + the same." Therefore, the universe is literally composed of the thoughts of the creator in verbal/informational form.
